heading: Judicial review of final action by Secretary on application
label: 6869 Judicial review of final action by Secretary on application
notes: Amendments1980—Subsecs. (a) to (c). substituted “Secretary” for “Administrator” wherever appearing, and “Secretary’s” for “Administrator’s”.Pub. L. 96–294
amendments: Amendments1980—Subsecs. (a) to (c). substituted “Secretary” for “Administrator” wherever appearing, and “Secretary’s” for “Administrator’s”.Pub. L. 96–294

children.heading: Time for appeal; jurisdiction; filing of administrative record by Secretary
children.label: a Time for appeal; jurisdiction; filing of administrative record by Secretary
children.text: If any applicant is dissatisfied with the Secretary’s final action with respect to the application submitted by it under or with a final action under , such applicant may, within 60 days after notice of such action, file with the United States court of appeals for the circuit in which the State involved is located a petition for review of that action. A copy of the petition shall be forthwith transmitted by the clerk of the court to the Secretary. The Secretary thereupon shall file in the court the record of the proceedings on which he based his action, as provided in .section 6864 of this titlesection 6868 of this titlesection 2112 of title 28

children.heading: Conclusiveness of findings of Secretary; remand; modified findings by Secretary; certification of record
children.label: b Conclusiveness of findings of Secretary; remand; modified findings by Secretary; certification of record
children.text: The findings of fact by the Secretary, if supported by substantial evidence, shall be conclusive. The court may, for good cause shown, remand the case to the Secretary to take further evidence, and the Secretary may thereupon make new or modified findings of fact and may modify his previous action. The Secretary shall certify to the court the record of any such further proceedings. Such new or modified findings of fact shall likewise be conclusive if supported by substantial evidence.

children.heading: Power of court to affirm or set aside action of Secretary; appeal to Supreme Court
children.label: c Power of court to affirm or set aside action of Secretary; appeal to Supreme Court
children.text: The court shall have jurisdiction to affirm the action of the Secretary or to set it aside, in whole or in part. The judgment of the court shall be subject to review by the Supreme Court of the United States upon certiorari or certification, as provided in .section 1254 of title 28
